figures.caption: Figure 4: Switchback example: The ratio of variance estimates to the true variance for all L ∈ {1, . . . , 20}.

Figure 3: Switchback example: We plot V̂ over V̂Bipartite across various (ℓ, b), where each V̂ is according to the best L among L ≤ 20. All variance estimates were conservative in this simulation, hence a ratio below 1 indicates when V̂ is tighter.

Table 1: Cycle example: True variance and estimated variances. HMS corresponds to Harshaw et al. [2026]. For NJ-avg and NJ-cov, we report the estimate at the selected block size L, which is the best among L ≤ 30.
Figure 2: Cycle example: The ratio of variance estimates to the true variance for all L ∈ {1, . . . , 30}.

Figure 1: An illustration of the DGP for the switchback example with T = 10000 and ℓ = 50. We plot time steps up to t = 1000. Each dotted vertical line marks the beginning of a new block. The dotted line in the plot for Yt traces τt ·Wt + ρ ·Ht. The shaded regions represent the burn-in parts with b = 25.
